source: Egypt Ministry of Health and Population (MoHP) child vaccination card (التطعيمات الأساسية للطفل); WHO EMRO Egypt country programme; UNICEF Egypt
source_notes: v2.1 (2026-04-26): re-anchored on the official MoHP child vaccination card (Arabic) saved at docs/sources/egypt_mohp_child_vaccination_card.pdf. The card mandates Pentavalent (DPT + HepB + Hib) at 2 / 4 / 6 months with a single IPV (شلل أطفال حقن) at 4 months — NOT Hexavalent at 2/4/6 as v2.0 modeled. v2.0's Hexavalent claim came from secondary WHO/UNICEF programme summaries that conflate public and private practice; the printed MoHP card is authoritative. OPV (شلل أطفال فموي) is given at birth, 2, 4, 6, 9, 12, and 18 months — v2.0 incorrectly placed OPV #1 at 1 month. HepB has a discrete birth dose (كبدي ب رضع at عند الميلاد); the engine flags this as `birth_dose: true` so later HepB-containing Pentavalent doses do not silently satisfy it. PCV, Rotavirus, Varicella, and Hepatitis A remain recommended/private. Clinically reviewed by Ahmed Zayed MD against the MoHP card.
scope: Routine childhood immunisation, birth to 72 months (6 years). Compulsory (EPI) and recommended (private) antigens with category distinction.
context_note: Destination-country schedule. In cross-border reconciliation INTO Egypt, incoming doses from a source country (e.g. Nigeria) are validated against this schedule — does each documented dose count under Egyptian EPI rules, what is missing, and what does the child need to enrol in an Egyptian nursery or school?
key_features: OPV is routine throughout Egyptian EPI (Egypt has been polio-free since 2006 but continues OPV) — given at birth, 2, 4, 6, 9, 12, and 18 months per the MoHP card

Pentavalent (DPT + HepB + Hib) at 2 / 4 / 6 months — Egypt's public EPI uses Pentavalent + a single separate IPV at 4 months, NOT Hexavalent. Private practice may use Hexavalent (Hexyon / Infanrix Hexa) which substitutes for both Pentavalent and IPV at the same visits.
Single IPV (شلل أطفال حقن) at 4 months — WHO/SAGE 2014 single-IPV transition policy
DPT uses DTPw (whole-cell pertussis) in public EPI; DTaP available privately
BCG within the first month (birth to 2 months window per the MoHP card)
HepB birth dose (كبدي ب رضع) is a discrete day-1 dose distinct from the Pentavalent HepB component — engine enforces this via `birth_dose: true`
Egypt transitioned measles-monovalent at 9m to MMR at 12m + 18m around 2020; pre-2020 cards may show measles-only at 9m
Yellow Fever is NOT routine in Egypt (non-endemic); arriving cards with YF should be preserved on record but not counted as missing
Rotavirus and PCV are recommended/private in Egypt; absence on an EPI card is expected, not a deficiency

doses.notes: Birth dose (كبدي ب رضع) — given on day 1 of life per the MoHP card. Discrete from the HepB component delivered via Pentavalent at 2/4/6 months. The birth dose protects against vertical HBV transmission and the engine requires a row with doseKind='birth' to credit it; later Pentavalent doses do NOT satisfy it.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP child vaccination card (PDF p. 1, row 1 at عند الميلاد)

doses.notes: OPV birth dose / zero-dose (شلل أطفال فموي at عند الميلاد) — given on day 1 alongside the HepB birth dose. v2.1 correction: v2.0 placed this at 1 month, but the MoHP card mandates birth.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP child vaccination card (PDF p. 1, row 2 at عند الميلاد)

doses.notes: BCG within the first month — the MoHP card lists it under 'من الميلاد حتى شهرين' (from birth until 2 months). Egyptian convention places it at the first well-child visit rather than at birth itself; either is accepted.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP child vaccination card (PDF p. 1, row at من الميلاد حتى شهرين)

doses.notes: Egyptian Pentavalent (الطعم الخماسي) at 2 months — covers Diphtheria, Pertussis, Tetanus, Viral Hepatitis B, and Hib. Egypt's public EPI uses Pentavalent + a separate single IPV at 4 months, NOT Hexavalent at 2/4/6. Private practice may substitute Hexavalent (Hexyon, Infanrix Hexa), which delivers the same components plus IPV in one shot.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP child vaccination card (PDF p. 1, شهرين row — full Arabic component list given)

doses.notes: OPV at 2 months, co-administered with Pentavalent.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP child vaccination card (PDF p. 1, شهرين row)

doses.notes: Pentavalent dose 2 at 4 months.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP child vaccination card (PDF p. 1, ٤ شهور row)

doses.notes: OPV at 4 months, co-administered with Pentavalent and IPV.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP child vaccination card (PDF p. 1, ٤ شهور row)

doses.notes: Single IPV dose (شلل أطفال حقن) at 4 months — WHO/SAGE 2014 single-IPV transition policy that Egypt and several other EMR countries adopted. The MoHP card lists IPV at 4 months only, not at 2 months. A card-side Hexavalent dose covers this IPV requirement (Hexavalent includes IPV); a card-side Pentavalent does not.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP child vaccination card (PDF p. 1, ٤ شهور row, line 'شلل أطفال (حقن)')

doses.notes: Pentavalent dose 3 at 6 months. Completes the public-EPI primary series for DPT/HepB/Hib.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP child vaccination card (PDF p. 1, ٦ شهور row)

doses.notes: OPV at 6 months, co-administered with Pentavalent dose 3.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP child vaccination card (PDF p. 1, ٦ شهور row)

doses.notes: OPV 4th dose at 9 months.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: MMR dose 1 at 12 months. Egypt transitioned from measles-monovalent to MMR; cards from children born before ~2020 may show measles-only at 9 months — that is a legacy record (see legacy_patterns).
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: OPV booster 1 at 12 months, co-administered with MMR dose 1.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: DPT booster (Diphtheria, Pertussis, Tetanus) at 18 months. Standalone DPT booster — does not include Hib or HepB. May appear as 'Tresivac' or 'DPT' on Egyptian cards.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: MMR dose 2 at 18 months. Minimum 4 weeks from dose 1.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: OPV booster 2 at 18 months.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: Diphtheria-Tetanus (DT) booster at school age (4–6 years). Does not include pertussis component.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: Recommended (not compulsory EPI). WHO-essential. PCV10 or PCV13. Variable uptake in public facilities; common in private practice.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt
Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt
Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: Recommended only — NOT compulsory EPI in Egypt. Available privately. Uptake is variable; typical Egyptian public-EPI cards will not show Varicella.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: Recommended second dose at 24 months. Minimum 4 weeks from dose 1.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

legacy_patterns.description: Cards from children vaccinated before Egypt's MMR transition (~2020) may show different patterns. The agent should recognise these as valid historical records.

patterns.maps_to: Partial MMR coverage — Measles component only. Mumps and Rubella not covered.
patterns.action: Count as Measles dose 1; flag Mumps and Rubella as unverified.

patterns.old_entry: Hexavalent (Hexyon / Infanrix Hexa) at 2/4/6 months on a private-practice card
patterns.maps_to: Pentavalent #1/2/3 + IPV #1 — Hexavalent delivers DPT+HepB+Hib+IPV in one shot
patterns.action: Credit each Hexavalent dose as covering the same-numbered Pentavalent dose; the IPV component of Hexavalent #2 covers the single IPV at 4 months.

interval_rules.notes: Egypt 2-4-6 month schedule; standard 8-week interval. ACIP minimum 28 days.
interval_rules.source: Egypt MoHP child vaccination card — 2-4-6 month schedule
Egypt MoHP child vaccination card — 2-4-6 month schedule
